io.github.steeb_k.Synchronicity.finish-args-home-filesystem-access: Synchronicity is a general-purpose rsync frontend. Users can type arbitrary local filesystem paths directly into the source and destination fields in addition to using the portal-backed file picker. Restricting to portal-only access would prevent manually entered paths from working, breaking a core workflow for users syncing directories outside portal-accessible locations.
io.github.steeb_k.Synchronicity.finish-args-has-socket-ssh-auth: rsync over SSH is a primary use case of the application. Without ssh-auth socket forwarding, passwordless SSH key authentication cannot be used inside the sandbox. Users would need to enter their key passphrase on every sync, making remote sync workflows significantly less usable.
